mnemonics: Most Ordinary Linear Homogeneous Recurrence Relations Are Constant
Order Matters In Expressions Rounded Around Constants
keyPoints: Definition: A second-order linear homogeneous recurrence relation with constant coefficients is an equation of the form: $a_n + b_n x_n + c_n y_n = 0$
Characteristics: Homogeneous, Linear, Second-Order, Constant Coefficients
Solutions: Can be expressed in terms of the roots of a quadratic equation, if the roots are distinct, or in terms of the exponential function, if the roots are repeated or complex
Examples: The Fibonacci sequence, The powers of a constant, etc.
